title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 32 32

questions.question: Choose the option that is not like the others.
questions.explanation: PDF, DOCX and PPTX are mainly used to store documents/presentations. JPEG is mainly used to store images/photos. Therefore, JPEG is the odd one out.

questions.question: What should come in the place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? BMV, JNP, ROJ, ?, HQX, PRR
questions.explanation: ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 26252423222120191817161514 ZYXWVUTSRQPON First letters: B + 8 = J, J + 8 = R, R + 8 = Z, Z + 8 = H, H + 8 = P Second letters: M + 1 = N, N + 1 = O, O + 1 = P, P + 1 = Q, Q + 1 = R Third letters: V − 6 = P, P − 6 = J, J − 6 = D, D − 6 = X, X − 6 = R So the missing term is Z R D = ZRD

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 30, 32, 28, 31, 25, 30, 22, ?
questions.explanation: 30 to 32 = +2 32 to 28 = −4 28 to 31 = +3 31 to 25 = −6 25 to 30 = +5 30 to 22 = −8 The series alternates between adding consecutive prime numbers (2, 3, 5, 7, ...) and subtracting consecutive even composite numbers (4, 6, 8, ...). So after 22, the next operation should be +7. 22 + 7 = 29 Answer: 29

questions.question: Choose the odd one out.
questions.explanation: R + 2 = T M + 2 = O B + 1 = C [Odd one out] S + 2 = U

questions.question: In a row of 42 candidates standing for a recruitment drive, Kabir is 13th from the left end and Naina is 17th from the right end. How many candidates are standing between Kabir and Naina?
questions.explanation: Naina is 17th from the right. Naina's position from the left = 42 − 17 + 1 = 26. Kabir is 13th from the left. Candidates between them = 26 − 13 − 1 = 12. Short trick: Number of candidates between Kabir and Naina = Total - (Left + Right) = 42 - (13 + 17) = 12

questions.question: If X Ψ Y means X is the father of B, X Ω Y means X is the wife of Y, and X ∞ Y means X is the sister of Y, then what does B Ω E Ψ G ∞ J mean?
options: B is the mother of J
B is the grandmother of J
B is the aunt of J
B is the mother-in-law of J
questions.explanation: B Ω E Ψ G ∞ J B Ω E means B is the wife of E E Ψ G means E is the father of G G ∞ J means G is the sister of J ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26445/image002.png) B is the mother of J.

questions.question: By interchanging the given two numbers which of the following equations will be correct? 13 and 50 I. 72 ÷ 4 + 8 x 50 - 13 = 70 II. 13 ÷ 10 - 34 + 50 x 6 = 49
questions.explanation: By interchanging the numbers 13 and 50, we get: I. 72 ÷ 4 + 8 x 13 - 50 = 70 = 18 + 104 - 50 = 122 - 50 = 72 So, equation I will be incorrect. II. 50 ÷ 10 - 34 + 13 x 6 = 49 = 5 - 34 + 78 = 83 - 34 = 49 So, equation II will be correct.

questions.question: Select the odd group of numbers. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed).
questions.explanation: Option (a): (97 - 85) X 2 = 12 X 2 = 24 Option (b): (76 - 41) X 2 = 35 X 2 = 70 Option (c): (62 - 39) X 2 = 23 X 2 = 46 Option (d): (59 - 23) X 2 = 36 X 2 = 72 Except option (c), all other options follow same logic.

questions.question: How many meaningful four-letter English words can be formed using the first, third, fourth and eighth letters (from left to right) of the word 'ASTRONOMY', using each letter only once in each word?
questions.explanation: Given word: ASTRONOMY The first, third, fourth and eighth letters of the word 'ASTRONOMY' = A, T, R, M The meaningful words formed are: TRAM, MART So, two meaningful four-letter English words can be formed. TRAM means a type of bus that works by electricity and that moves along special rails in the road. MART means a trade centre or market.

questions.question: Taxpayer's refund failed due to an unvalidated bank account. Return is e-verified. CPC rules allow reissue after bank pre-validation and re-mandate; timeline window remains open. What should be done?
options: Release refund to the old account
Refer to CPC to trigger bank pre-validation, then place a refund reissue request
Wait until the window closes and file a condonation later
questions.explanation: Refund reissue requires bank pre-validation => current account must be validated first. The reissue window is open => immediate action is permissible. Referring to CPC for pre-validation followed by reissue aligns with process and preserves timelines.

questions.question: In the question below, a statement is given followed by a few conclusions which may or may not follow from the statement. Choose the one that reflects the conclusion that follows. Statement: An official notification for the application for the posts of Sub-inspector stated, "Hindi Typing Speed of 25 WPM (words per minute) & English Typing 30 WPM is required." Conclusions: I. It is easier to type English words than Hindi words. II. There will be work for Sub-inspectors that involves typing. III. Typing skill is a must for all government jobs.
options: None of I, II and III follows
questions.explanation: Since the application says that typing skill is required, so we can conclude that the work of the sub-inspector will require some typing. We cannot conclude that typing English is easier than typing Hindi though we may state (derived from the statement) that typing English is faster than typing Hindi. Also, we cannot conclude that all government jobs require typing skills as the statement is an official notification of the post of Sub-inspector only. Hence, (b) is correct.

questions.question: In the following question, two statements are given and two conclusions are given below these statements. Choose the option which shows the conclusions which logically follow from the given statements, disregarding the commonly known facts. Statements: Only a few Sauce is Pulp All Sauce is Ketchup Conclusions: I. Some Ketchup is not Pulp II. Some Ketchup is Sauce
options: Either conclusion I or II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow
questions.explanation: Statements: Only a few Sauce is Pulp All Sauce is Ketchup ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26322/image002.png) Some Ketchup is not Pulp: Only a few Sauce is Pulp means some Sauce is Pulp and some Sauce is not Pulp. All Sauce is Ketchup. Thus, The Ketchup which is Sauce is not Pulp. So, conclusion I is true. Some Ketchup is Sauce: All Sauce is Ketchup. Thus, some Ketchup is Sauce. So, conclusion II is true. Hence, Both conclusions I and II follow.

questions.question: Select the number set from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. (64 : 9), (81 : 10), (225 : 16), ?

Read the given statement and conclusions carefully. Decide which of the given conclusions is/are definitely true from the statement. Statement: T = F > B ≤ P = G Conclusion I: F < G Conclusion II: T > P
options: Neither conclusion I nor conclusion II is true
Both conclusion I and conclusion II are true
questions.explanation: It is given that, T = F > B ≤ P = G Conclusion I: F < G does not follow as the relationship between F and G cannot be determined. Conclusion II: T > P does not follow as the relationship between T and P cannot be determined. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Read the situation and choose the best judgment: A ride-hailing driver asks you to cancel the app trip and pay cash so they can "avoid platform fees." The car has already arrived, and you are in a hurry.
options: Agree, pay cash, and ask for a handwritten receipt.
Complete the ride on the app, then complain later if needed.
Start recording the driver and threaten to post it online.
Decline the off-app request, do not board, and report through the in-app safety/help channel with car details.
questions.explanation: Using the official channel prevents fare fraud and preserves safety and insurance coverage. Not boarding avoids participation in a policy breach. Recording or threatening escalation can inflame the situation and still leaves the breach unreported. Completing the ride first weakens evidence and may normalize the misconduct.

questions.question: Select the term that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term. Candle: Flame :: Bulb: ?
questions.explanation: As Candle has a Flame. Similarly, Bulb has a Light. So, Option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Study the given pattern carefully and select the number that can replace the question mark(?) in it. (36,8,2),(121,16,?),(100,25,15)
questions.explanation: The first number is square of the difference of second and third number. So, 16-5=11 11²= 121.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number. 5 : 30 :: 9 : 90 :: 7 : ?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 5²+5= 30; 9²+9= 90. Therefore, 7²+7= 56.

questions.question: Which letter-cluster will replace the question mark (?) to complete the given series? IVLS, GWKQ, ?, CYIM, AZHK
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: For 1ˢᵗ letter of each term: I-2= G; G-2= E; E-2= C; C-2= A. For 2ⁿᵈ letter of each term: V+1=W; W+1= X; X+1= Y; Y+1= Z. For 3ʳᵈ letter of each term: L-1= K; K-1= J; J-1= I; I-1= H. For 4ᵗʰ letter of each term: S-2= Q; Q-2= O; O-2= M; M-2= K.

questions.question: If '!!' denotes '÷', '&&' denotes '+', '%%' denotes ' X ' and '@@' denotes '-', then what will come in place of '?' in the following equation? 78 !! 13 %% 10 @@ 25 && 9 = ?
Which of the following article deals with the protection against arrest and detention in certain areas?
questions.explanation: Article 22 of the Indian Constitution deals with protection against arrest and detention in certain cases. It provides for certain rights for individuals who are arrested or detained. These include: The right to be informed of the grounds of arrest. The right to consult and be defended by a legal practitioner of one's choice. The right to be produced before the nearest magistrate within 24 hours of arrest (excluding journey time). The right not to be detained in custody beyond the said period without the authority of a magistrate. Article 22 also deals with preventive detention, laying down certain safeguards for persons detained under preventive detention laws. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Article 21: Guarantees the Protection of Life and Personal Liberty. It states that "No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law." (b) Article 21A: Guarantees the Right to Education. It states that "The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years in such manner as the State may, by law, determine." (Added by the 86th Amendment Act, 2002). (d) Article 19: Guarantees six fundamental freedoms: (a) freedom of speech and expression, (b) freedom to assemble peaceably and without arms, (c) freedom to form associations or unions or co-operative societies, (d) freedom to move freely throughout the territory of India, (e) freedom to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India, and (g) freedom to practise any profession, or to carry on any occupation, trade or business. (Clause (f) regarding right to property was omitted). Further insights: The safeguards provided under Article 22 are not available to an enemy alien or to a person arrested or detained under a law providing for preventive detention (though separate safeguards apply for preventive detention cases). Preventive detention means detention of a person without trial and conviction by a court, on the mere suspicion that they might commit some wrongful act. Article 22(4) to (7) lay down the procedural safeguards against arbitrary preventive detention.

questions.question: Green revolution refers to the large increase in production of food grains resulting from the use of: I. High yielding variety (HYV) seeds II. Fertilizers and pesticides III. Regular supply of water Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
questions.explanation: Green revolution refers to the large increase in production of food grains resulting from the use of high yielding variety (HYV) seeds especially for wheat and rice. The use of these seeds required the use of fertiliser and pesticide in the correct quantities as well as regular supply of water; the application of these inputs in correct proportions is vital. The farmers who could benefit from HYV seeds required reliable irrigation facilities as well as the financial resources to purchase fertiliser and pesticide. As a result, in the first phase of the green revolution (approximately mid 1960s upto mid 1970s), the use of HYV seeds was restricted to the more affluent states such as Punjab, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu. Further, the use of HYV seeds primarily benefited the wheat growing regions only.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Cash Reserve Ratio: I. It is an interest-bearing deposit of banks with RBI. II. An increase in the CRR will help contain the inflation in the economy. Select the correct statements.
questions.explanation: Cash Reserve Ratio refers to the fraction of the total Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL) of a Commercial Bank held in India, that it maintains as cash deposit with the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). Banks are not paid any interest on behalf of the RBI for parking the required cash. But, If a bank fails to meet its required reserve requirements, the RBI is empowered to impose a penalty by charging a penal interest rate. Hence, Statement 1 is incorrect. During high levels of inflation, attempts are made to reduce the money supply in the economy. For this, RBI increases the CRR, sucking the loanable funds available with the banks. This, in turn, slows down investment and reduces the supply of money in the economy. As a result, the growth of the economy is negatively impacted. However, this also helps bring down inflation. Hence, Statement 2 is correct. Source: NCERT

questions.question: Identify the incorrect statement regarding Olympic swimming pool requirement. I. The pool measures 50 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a minimum depth of 2 meters. II. There are typically 10 lanes, each lane being 2.5 meters wide.
questions.explanation: According to FINA rules World Championships require 8 lanes and Olympic Games require 10 lanes. The lanes are a minimum of 2.5m wide, with two spaces of at least 2.5m wide outside of the first and last lanes. The International Swimming Federation, also known as the Fdration Internationale de Natation (FINA), which oversees open-water swimming, synchronized swimming, swimming, and water polo, sets the rigorous guidelines for the Olympics' competitive long pools. Key Dimensions (Long Course Pool) Length: 50 meters (164 feet) Width: 25 meters (82 feet) Depth: A minimum of 2 meters (6.6 feet) is required, but a depth of 3 meters (9.8 feet) is recommended to reduce water turbulence. Lane Specifications Number of Lanes: The pool must have 10 lanes. Lanes Used for Racing: Only the middle 8 lanes are used during competitions. Buffer Lanes: The outer two lanes (lane 1 and lane 10) are left empty to act as a buffer zone, absorbing waves and ensuring fairer conditions for the swimmers in the outer racing lanes. Lane Width: Each lane must be 2.5 meters (8.2 feet) wide. Environmental Conditions Water Temperature: Must be strictly maintained between 25C and 28C (77F - 82.4F). Lighting: A minimum brightness level of 1500 lux is required over the entire pool.

questions.question: Identify the incorrect statement. A. Gol Gumbad is situated in Bijapur in the Bijapur District of Andhra Pradesh. B. It is the mausoleum of Muhammad Adil Shah.
questions.explanation: • Gol Gumbad(also written as Gol Gumbaz) is situated in Bijapur in the Bijapur District of Karnataka. • It is the mausoleum of Muhammad Adil Shah (1626 - 1656), the seventh Sultan of the Adil Shahi Dynasty of Bijapur (1489 - 1686). • Built by the ruler himself, it is a striking edifice in spite of being unfinished. • The tomb is a complex of buildings such as a gateway, a Naqqar Khana, a mosque and a sarai located within a large-walled garden. • The Gumbad is a monumental square building topped with a circular drum over which rests a majestic dome, giving the building its nomenclature. • It is built of dark gray basalt and decorated plasterwork. Each wall of the tomb is one hundred and thirty-five feet long and one hundred and ten feet high and ten feet thick. • With the drum and the dome, the building rises to a height of over two hundred feet. • The tomb has only one square chamber and the dome, with a diameter of one hundred and twenty-five feet, it covers an uninterrupted floor space of 18,337 square feet, the second largest in the world.

questions.question: With reference to 'Saila dance' consider the following statements and select the correct option. a) A group comprising of male performers. b) The dance is performed with bamboo sticks. c) It is performed after the cutting of crops.
questions.explanation: The Saila dance is a tribal folk dance of Chhattisgarh, especially among the Gond and other tribal communities. It is performed: By male dancers With bamboo sticks, forming rhythmic formations During post-harvest celebrations, symbolizing joy and gratitude

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Deccan Plateau- 1) The highest peak of peninsular plateau is Anaimudi which is located on Nilgiri Hills. 2) Western Ghats are comparatively lower in elevation and more discontinuous than the Eastern Ghats. Select the correct answer using the codes given below-
questions.explanation: The highest peak of peninsular plateau is Anaimudi (2695 mtr) which is located on Anaimalai hills of the Western Ghats, followed by Dodabetta (2637 mtr) on the Nilgiri Hills. Western Ghats are comparatively higher in elevation and more continuous than the Eastern Ghats. Their average elevation is about 1500 m with the height increasing from North to South. Eastern ghats comprising the discontinuous and low hills are highly eroded by the rivers such as the Mahanadi, the Godavari, the Krishna, the Kavari etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are considered as Revenue Expenditure? i. Salaries and Pensions ii. Subsidies iii. Grant-in-aid iv. Infrastructure development
questions.explanation: Article 112: Annual Financial Statement · Union budget is categories into two main component (Revenue Budget and Capital Budget) and each of them further classified into two subcomponents. (Revenue Receipts + Revenue Expenditure and Capital Receipts + Capital Expenditure respectively) Revenue Receipts • It estimates the amount or revenue government expects to receive form the citizens through Tax Revenue (Income tax, corporate tax etc.) and Non-tax Revenue (such as Interest received, profits, Penalties etc.) Revenue Expenditure • These are the expenses incur by the government for their daily function. • It includes salaries and pension of their employees, subsidies to the citizens and expenses on providing essential public services and facilities. Capital Receipts • This is the money government acquire via loans from public, Banks, Central Bank and other countries • These are liabilities government received and had to return one day Capital Expenditure • These are the expenses the government incurs to create long-term asset and expect to receive returns from it in future. And to provide welfare facilities to the citizens • It includes constructing road, constructing schools and colleges, hospital and granting loans to states and union territories. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25697/image001.png)

questions.question: Which of the following incidents is/are associated with the formation of the 'Indian National Congress'? I. First meeting was convened at Poona, Maharashtra. II. It was founded by W.C. Banerjee III. First meeting was presided over by Allen Hume. Select the correct code.
questions.explanation: Indian National Congress was founded by Allen O. Hume after consultations with Lord Dufferin. He was a retired Government servant who had chosen to stay back in India after retirement. So, statement II is not correct. In March 1885, it was decided that a conference of the Indian National Union (old name of INC) would be convened at Poona during December month of the year. However, fate deprived Poona of the opportunity to host the first session of the Indian National Congress. The venue had to be shifted to Bombay because of the outbreak of cholera in Poona. So, statement I is not correct. The first meeting was held on Monday, 28 December 1885 in Gokaldas Tejpal Sanskrit College, Bombay. The honour of being the first ever Congress President belonged to W.C. Banerjee of Bengal. He was one of the first four Indian Barristers and one of the foremost legal luminaries in his day. So, statement III is not correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct chemical representation of Butane?
questions.explanation: Butane or n-butane is an alkane with the formula C₄H₁₀. Butane is a gas at room temperature and atmospheric pressure. Butane is a highly flammable, colourless, easily liquefied gas that quickly evaporates at room temperature. Explanation of Other Options: (a) C₂H₅: This is the formula for the ethyl group (an alkyl radical), not a stable molecule. Ethane is C₂H₆. (c) C₃H₆: This is the molecular formula for Propene (an alkene) or Cyclopropane (a cycloalkane). Propane (alkane) is C₃H₈. (d) C₆H₁₀: This molecular formula could represent various unsaturated hydrocarbons like Hexyne (an alkyne) or cyclic compounds like Cyclohexene. Hexane (alkane) is C₆H₁₄. Further insights: Butane has two isomers: n-butane (a straight-chain alkane) and isobutane (2-methylpropane, a branched-chain alkane). Both have the same molecular formula C₄H₁₀. Butane is a highly flammable gas commonly used as a fuel, particularly in lighters, portable stoves, and as a propellant in aerosol sprays.

questions.question: Percentage of India's rural population as per Census 2011?
questions.explanation: • Rural population made up 68.8% of India's total population. Further Insight: • Total Population: 1,210,569,573 (1.21 billion) ○ Males: 623.7 million (51.54%) ○ Females: 586.46 million (48.46%) ○ Rural Population: 833 million (68.8%) ○ Urban Population: 377 million (31.2%) • Decadal Population Growth (2001-2011): 17.64% ○ Increase in Population: 181 million • Population Density: 382 people per square kilometer

questions.question: Arrange the following Governor generals of India in Chronological order: I. Lord Wellesley II. Lord Cornwallis III. Lord Minto I IV. Lord Hastings. Select the correct answer from the code given below:
questions.explanation: Warren Hastings 1773-1785 Lord Cornwallis 1786-1793 Sir John Shore 1793-1798 Lord Wellesley 1798-1805 Sir George Barlow 1805-1807 Lord Minto I 1807-1813 Lord Hastings 1813-1823 Lord Amherst 1823-1828 Lord William Bentinck 1828-1835 Lord Metcalfe 1835-1836 Lord Auckland 1836-1842 Lord Ellenborough 1842-1844 Lord Hardinge I 1844-1848 Lord Dalhousie 1848-1856 Lord Canning 1856-1857 Source: A Brief History of Modern India, Rajiv Ahir.

questions.question: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences was awarded for contributions related to:
options: Innovation-driven economic growth and creative destruction
The role of institutions in long-term economic development
Behavioural economics and decision-making under uncertainty
Inequality and redistribution in developing economies
questions.explanation: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences was awarded to Joel Mokyr, Philippe Aghion, and Peter Howitt for their work on innovation-driven economic growth, including Mokyr's historical analysis of technological progress and Aghion-Howitt's theory of creative destruction. · 'Over the last two centuries, for the first time in history, the world has seen sustained economic growth. This has lifted vast numbers of people out of poverty and laid the foundation of our prosperity. This year's laureates in economic sciences... explain how innovation provides the impe'tus for further progress,' said The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences, which is responsible for selecting Nobel laureates in physics, chemistry and economic sciences. · The economics prize is formally known as the Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences and is presented along with other Nobel Prizes on December 10 in Stockholm, Sweden. · Economic historian Mokyr, the committee said, 'Used historical sources as one means to uncover the causes of sustained growth becoming the new normal.' He stressed that in order for innovations to succeed one another in a self-generating process, it is not enough to know that something works, but understanding the scientific reasons why it works is also essential. Before the industrial revolution, such explanations were often missing, making it difficult to build on new discoveries and inventions, the committee said. · Meanwhile, Aghion and Howitt developed a mathematical model of 'creative destruction', describing it as a process in which new and better products enter the market, causing companies selling older products to lose out, the committee said. · They argued that creative destruction creates conflicts that must be managed in a constructive manner. Otherwise, established companies and interest groups threatened by change can hinder innovation. · John Hassler, chair of the Committee for the prize, said, 'The laureates' work shows that economic growth cannot be taken for granted. We must uphold the mechanisms that underly creative destruction, so that we do not fall back into stagnation,' · Last year, the Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences was awarded to US-based scholars Simon Johnson, James Robinson, and Daron Acemoglu for their research examining how colonisation and the establishment of public institutions help explain why some countries have remained trapped in poverty for decades.

questions.question: Who is first Indian rower to win a gold at the Asian Games in the year 2010?
questions.explanation: Bajrang Lal Takhar was the first Indian rower to win an individual gold medal at the Asian Games. He achieved this feat in the Men's Single Sculls event at the 2010 Asian Games held in Guangzhou, China. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Sawarn Singh: An Indian rower who has won medals at the Asian Games (e.g., bronze in single sculls in 2014, gold in quadruple sculls in 2018), but Bajrang Lal Takhar was the first to win an individual gold in 2010. (c) Dattu Baban Bhokanal: An Indian rower who has represented India in the Olympics and won medals at Asian Championships and Asian Games (e.g., gold in quadruple sculls in 2018). (d) Dushyant Chauhan (often just Dushyant): An Indian rower who has won medals at the Asian Games (e.g., bronze in lightweight single sculls in 2014 and 2018). Further insights: Bajrang Lal Takhar's gold medal in 2010 was a landmark achievement for Indian rowing. He had previously won a silver medal in the same event at the 2006 Asian Games in Doha. Rowing has been a discipline in which Indian athletes have consistently performed well at the Asian Games.

questions.question: The India Post has rolled out Advanced Postal Technology across the country that will transform it into world-class public logistics organization by enabling mobile-ready services and real time decision making, in August 2025. Identify the total investment outlay for the Advanced Postal Technology (APT) initiative.
questions.explanation: The India Post has rolled out Rs 5,800-crore Advanced Postal Technology across the country that will transform it into world-class public logistics organization by enabling mobile-ready services and real time decision making, Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said. · The Advanced Postal Technology (APT) based infrastructure gives India Post access to new technology layers like modern logistics companies. · The technology platform will enable India Post to support full fledged digital transactions and accept UPI payments for services from customers of any bank. · Earlier, UPI transactions of only India Post Payments Bank accounts were supported at post offices for purchase of services and other payments due to technical issues. · The minister said the new technology will "Enable real-time decision making, boost e-commerce reach, cut operational costs through automation and deliver citizen-first, mobile-ready services anywhere-anytime.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is correct about Isotope?
options: Isotopes have the same physical properties
Isotopes have the same chemical properties
Isotopes have the same number of Neutrons
questions.explanation: The correct statement about isotopes is: (b) Isotopes have the same chemical properties. Isotopes are atoms of the same chemical element that have the same number of protons (same atomic number, Z) but different numbers of neutrons, and therefore different mass numbers (A). Since the number of protons (and thus electrons in a neutral atom) determines the chemical behavior of an element (as chemical properties depend on electron configuration), isotopes of an element generally exhibit the same chemical properties. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Isotopes have the same physical properties: This is generally incorrect. Isotopes have different masses due to the different number of neutrons. This difference in mass can lead to slight variations in physical properties such as density, melting point, boiling point, and rate of diffusion (e.g., kinetic isotope effect). (c) Isotopes have the same number of Neutrons: This is incorrect. The defining characteristic of isotopes is that they have different numbers of neutrons (while having the same number of protons). (d) Isotopes have different atomic numbers: This is incorrect. Isotopes of the same element have the same atomic number (number of protons). If the atomic number were different, they would be different elements. Further insights: For example, Carbon-12 (⁶C: 6 protons, 6 neutrons) and Carbon-14 (⁶C⁴: 6 protons, 8 neutrons) are isotopes of carbon. Both have 6 protons and thus similar chemical properties (e.g., forming similar compounds like CO₂), but C-14 is heavier and radioactive, while C-12 is stable. The slight differences in physical properties due to mass differences are utilized in isotope separation techniques. Radioactive isotopes (radioisotopes) have unstable nuclei and undergo radioactive decay, making them useful in various applications like medical imaging, radiometric dating, and research.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the art and craft of the Indus Valley Civilization: (a) The famous Dancing Girl figurine found in Mohenjodaro was made of bronze. (b) Faience was used to make beads and bangles. (c) The bearded Priest-King statue was made of terracotta. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Let's analyze each statement: (a) The famous Dancing Girl figurine found in Mohenjodaro was made of bronze. This statement is correct. The iconic "Dancing Girl" statuette, discovered in Mohenjodaro in 1926, is a masterpiece of Harappan art. It was created using the sophisticated lost-wax (cire perdue) casting technique and is made of bronze. (b) Faience was used to make beads and bangles. This statement is correct. Faience, a non-clay ceramic made of powdered quartz, was considered a luxury material in the Indus Valley Civilization. It was used to produce various small, decorative objects, including beads, bangles, amulets, and miniature vessels. (c) The bearded Priest-King statue was made of terracotta. This statement is incorrect. The famous bust of a bearded man, often called the "Priest-King," was also found at Mohenjodaro. However, it is carved from steatite (soapstone), not terracotta. Terracotta was widely used for other items like toys, seals, and figurines (such as the Mother Goddess), but this specific statue is made of stone. Since statements (a) and (b) are correct and statement (c) is incorrect, the correct option is (b).

questions.question: Which of the following statements are Correct? I. National Waterway-2 : Connects Allahabad to Haldia II. National Waterway-3 : Connects Kottapuram to Kollam III. National Waterway-4 : Connects Kakinada to Puducherry Choose the Correct option
questions.explanation: Details on National Waterways: National waterway 1: Ganga-Bhagirathi-Hooghly River System (Haldia - Allahabad) National waterway 2: Brahmaputra River (Dhubri - Sadiya) National Waterway 3: West Coast Canal (Kottapuram - Kollam), Champakara and Udyogmandal Canals National Waterway 4: Krishna River (Vijayawada - Muktyala), connects Kakinada to Puducherry National Waterway 5: Dhamra-Paradio via Mangalagadi to Pankopal National Waterway 8: Alappuzha- Changanassery Canal National Waterway 9: Alappuzha-Kottayam Athirampuzha Canal

questions.question: Which of the following statement is/are true regarding Khelo India Youth Games 2025? a. Maharashtra defended their Khelo India Youth Games title after topping the medals tally. b. Bihar played host to the majority of events and competitions.
questions.explanation: Khelo India Youth Games 2025, the seventh edition of the flagship event under the Indian government's Khelo India initiative, concluded on May 15. Over 5,000 young athletes from all 36 States and Union Territories across India competed for medals in 27 sports in KIYG 2025, which ran from May 3 to 15. Esports was also a part of KIYG 2025, but only as a demonstration sport. Bihar played host to the majority of events and competitions were held in Patna, Rajgir, Gaya, Bhagalpur and Begusarai. New Delhi hosted the shooting, gymnastics and track cycling events. Maharashtra defended their Khelo India Youth Games title after topping the medals tally with 58 gold medals, 47 silver and 53 bronze. It was their fifth KIYG title and the third on the trot. Maharashtra won gold medals across 14 sports, with a bulk of them coming from athletics (10), swimming (seven), gymnastics (seven), archery (six) and weightlifting (five). Haryana, who have won two KIYG titles, finished second with 39 gold medals, 27 silver and 51 bronze, while Rajasthan were third with 24 gold, 12 silver and 24 bronze medals. Meanwhile, hosts Bihar finished in 15th position after winning seven gold medals, 11 silver and 18 bronze.

questions.question: A substantial increase in capital expenditure or revenue deficit leads to ______.
questions.explanation: A substantial increase in either capital expenditure or revenue deficit (which contributes to total expenditure) will lead to an increase in the (d) Fiscal Deficit, assuming other factors remain constant. Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure - Total Receipts (excluding borrowings) If capital expenditure increases, total expenditure increases. If revenue deficit increases (meaning revenue expenditure is much higher than revenue receipts), this also increases total expenditure or reduces the non-borrowing receipts available to finance total expenditure. Both scenarios widen the gap between total expenditure and total non-borrowing receipts, thus increasing the fiscal deficit, which represents the government's total borrowing requirement. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Budgetary Deficit: In older terminology, budgetary deficit referred to the difference between total expenditure and total receipts (both revenue and capital). Modern fiscal analysis primarily uses the concept of fiscal deficit. If revenue deficit increases, and this is not offset by a capital surplus, the overall deficit (fiscal deficit) would increase. (b) Revenue Deficit: An increase in capital expenditure does not directly lead to an increase in revenue deficit (Revenue Deficit = Revenue Expenditure - Revenue Receipts). However, an increase in revenue deficit itself is one of the conditions mentioned. (c) Primary Deficit: Primary Deficit = Fiscal Deficit - Interest Payments. While an increase in fiscal deficit will likely increase the primary deficit (unless interest payments decrease proportionally, which is unlikely if the fiscal deficit is rising due to increased expenditure), the direct and encompassing consequence of increased capital expenditure or revenue deficit is an increase in the fiscal deficit. Further insights: Capital Expenditure: Spending on creation of assets like infrastructure, machinery, etc. Revenue Deficit: Occurs when revenue expenditure exceeds revenue receipts. A high fiscal deficit implies a greater need for the government to borrow, which can lead to increased public debt, higher interest payments in the future, and potential inflationary pressures. Managing the fiscal deficit is a key objective of government fiscal policy.

questions.question: 'Golden duck' and 'Hook' are related to which sports?
questions.explanation: Some terms related to cricket are: Golden duck: When a batsman is out first ball. Maiden: When a bowler delivers an entire over without the batsman managing to score a run. Yorker: A delivery that is intended to pitch directly underneath a batsman's bat or on his toes. Hook: A hook is a shot taken when the ball is pitched outside the leg stump and the ball comes up to the batsman at shoulder height.

questions.question: The Mahalanobis strategy of planning gave emphasis to the growth of
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis strategy of planning, which formed the basis of India's Second Five-Year Plan (1956-1961), gave strong emphasis to the growth of Heavy Industries. This strategy, developed by Professor P.C. Mahalanobis, advocated for rapid industrialization by focusing investment on the capital goods sector (industries that produce machinery and equipment for other industries) to build a self-reliant and strong industrial base for the country. Further insights: The Mahalanobis model is a two-sector (later expanded to four-sector) growth model that analyzes the allocation of investment between the capital goods sector and the consumer goods sector. This strategy led to the establishment of major public sector enterprises in steel, heavy engineering, machinery, etc., during the Second Plan period. The goal was to achieve long-term economic growth and self-sufficiency through import-substitution industrialization.

questions.question: "Thodu Bommalata" (String Puppet) is a traditional art form of which state?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh is the state associated with Thodu Bommalata. Thodu Bommalata, also known as String Puppet theatre, is a traditional puppet art form from Andhra Pradesh. The puppets are controlled by strings and narrate stories primarily from Hindu epics like Ramayana and Mahabharata.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement.
options: Joule is the unit of work.
Newton is a unit of power
Newton is the unit of work
Joule is the unit of power
questions.explanation: • Joule is the unit of work. • Newton is the unit of force. Further Insight: Physical QuantityUnit LengthMeter (m) MassKilogram (kg) TimeSecond (s) Electric CurrentAmpere (A) TemperatureKelvin (K) Luminous IntensityCandela (cd) Amount of SubstanceMole (mol) ForceNewton (N) WorkJoule (J) PowerWatt (W) PressurePascal (Pa) EnergyJoule (J)

questions.question: Match the following : 1. Pitts India Acta. 1773 2. Simon Commissionb. 1784 3. Regulating Actc. 1942 4. Cripps Missiond. 1927
questions.explanation: Pitts India Act - b. 1784: Pitt's India Act was passed by the British Parliament in 1784 to bring the East India Company's rule in India under greater control of the British government. Simon Commission - d. 1927: The Simon Commission, a group of seven British Members of Parliament, was appointed in 1927 to study constitutional reform in British India. Regulating Act - a. 1773: The Regulating Act of 1773 was an act of the Parliament of Great Britain intended to overhaul the management of the East India Company's rule in India. Cripps Mission - c. 1942: The Cripps Mission was an attempt in March 1942 by the British government to secure full Indian cooperation and support for their efforts in World War II. Therefore, the correct combination is (c) 1-b, 2-d, 3-a, 4-c. Further insights: Each of these acts and commissions represents a significant milestone in the history of British rule in India and the Indian independence movement. The Regulating Act and Pitt's India Act were early attempts to assert British parliamentary control over the East India Company. The Simon Commission was met with widespread protests in India ("Go Back Simon") because it included no Indian members. The Cripps Mission's proposals were ultimately rejected by the Indian National Congress and other political parties for various reasons, including the offer of dominion status instead of complete independence.

questions.question: Which of these number sets is NOT a subset of real numbers?
questions.explanation: Real numbers include rational numbers and irrational numbers. Option (A): Irrational numbers are real numbers, so they are a subset of real numbers Option (B): Integers are real numbers, so they are a subset of real numbers. Option (C): Pure imaginary numbers are of the form bi where i² = −1 and b ≠ 0. Such numbers are not real (they lie on the imaginary axis in the complex plane). Therefore, pure imaginary numbers are not a subset of real numbers. Option (D): Natural numbers are real numbers, so they are a subset of real numbers.

questions.question: An article is sold at a profit of 131_________4 %. Had it been sold for ₹76.70 more, the profit would have been 161_________5 %. 50% of the cost price of the article (in ₹) is:

questions.question: A and B completed a work in 5 days. Had A worked at twice his efficiency and B at half his efficiency, they would have finished the work in 4 days. In how many days can A alone finish the work?
questions.explanation: Suppose A and B take A and B days respectively to complete the work on their own. 1/A + 1/B = ⅕ (1) If A works at double efficiency he would take A/2 days and similarly B will take 2B days if he works at half his efficiency. According to the condition, 2/A + 1/2B = ¼ => 4/A + 1/B = ½ (2) (2) - (1) => 3/A = 3/10 => A = 10 days

questions.question: Point M is the centre of a circle and GHKL is a cyclic quadrilateral. If side GL is extended to point N such that LN is equal to LK, and ∠GHK and ∠HGL are 68° and 78°, respectively, then find the value of ∠HKN.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p15712/image005.jpg) As, GHKL is a cyclic quadrilateral and ∠GHK and ∠HGL are 68° and 78°, respectively So, ∠GLK = 180° - 68° = 112° And ∠LKH = 180° - 78° = 102° For Straight line GLN is 180° So, ∠NLK = 180° - 112° = 68° Since LN = LK. So, ∆KLN is an isosceles triangle. Thus, ∠LNK = ∠LKN Hence, ∠LKN = (180° - 68°)/2 = 56° Therefore, ∠HKN = ∠HKL + ∠LKN = 56° + 102° = 158°

questions.question: Find the amount received by Nayan after 18 months on ₹1024 at 25% rate of interest compounded half yearly.
A train runs 160 km at 80 km/h. Owing to a caution order, its speed is reduced by 25% for the next 120 km. What is the total time taken for the entire journey in minutes?
questions.explanation: Reduced speed = 75% of 80 = 60 km/h Time for first 160 km = 160/80 = 2 hours Time for next 120 km = 120/60 = 2 hours Total time = (2 + 2)*60 = 240 minutes

questions.question: If the curved surface area of a right circular cylinder is 2904 cm² and its volume is 30492 cm³, what are its radius and height respectively? (π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Let r = radius, h = height. CSA = 2πrh = 2904 Volume = πr²h = 30492 CSA/Volume = 2/r = 2904/30492 = ⅒.5 r = 21 cm From the given options, only c has the radius equal to 21 cm.So, height will be 22 cm. Hence option c is correct.

questions.question: Nidhi and Sunidhi entered into a partnership by investing their amount in the ratio of 12:17 respectively. Nidhi invested for 18 months and Sunidhi invested for 24 months. If they earned ₹16250 as profit after two years, then the profit share of Sunidhi will be -
questions.explanation: Ratio of profit of Nidhi and Sunidhi = (12*18):(17*24) = 9:17 Total profit = ₹16250 So, profit share of Sunidhi = 16250*17/(9 + 17) = ₹10625

questions.question: A police jeep begins chasing a taxi which is 20 km ahead of it. The speeds of the police jeep and the taxi are 96 km/h and 56 km/h respectively. If the police jeep catches the taxi in p minutes, then find the distance covered by the taxi in (p + 15) minutes.
questions.explanation: Relative speed in the same direction = speed of police jeep - speed of taxi Relative speed = 96 - 56 = 40 km/h Time taken to catch = initial gap / relative speed p = 20/40 hour = 30 minutes (p + 15) minutes = 45 minutes = ¾ hour Distance covered by taxi = 56 X ¾ = 42 km

questions.question: If one day on planet X is of 75 hours, then 15 minutes on it can be expressed as ____?
questions.explanation: According to Question, One day on Planet X = 75 hours =4500 minutes 15 Minutes on planet X = 15/4500 = 1/300 Therefore, 15 minutes on a planet as a fraction of 75 hours is 1/300 Ans.

questions.question: If A: B = 4_________5 : 8_________9 , and B: C = 2: 3, then what is the value of 2A + 3B_________4A - 3C
What are the coordinates of the point common to the lines 5x - 4y = 8 and 2x + 7y = 29?
questions.explanation: Linear equations: 5x - 4y = 8 -------(1) 2x + 7y = 29 ------(2) 2*eqn(1) - 5*eqn(2): (10x - 8y) - (10x + 35y) = 16 - 145 43y = 129 y = 3 Putting the value of y in eqn(2) - 2x + 7*3 = 29 2x = 8 x = 4 Points of coordinate = (4, 3)

questions.question: The perimeter of a sector of a circle is 77 cm and the radius is 11 cm. Calculate the area (in sq. cm) of the sector.
questions.explanation: Arc length = Perimeter of sector - Diameter = 77 - 22 = 55 cm Area of sector = (½) x arc length x radius = (½) x 55 x 11 = 302.5 sq. cm

questions.question: Find the simple interest on Rs.4000 at 12½ % per annum for the period from 4ᵗʰ Feb 2017 to 17ᵗʰ April 2017.
questions.explanation: Since there are 28 days in February in 2017. There are 25 days from 4ᵗʰ feb to 28ᵗʰ feb Time = (25 + 31 + 17)days = 73 days = 73/365 year = ⅕ year Now, principle = 4000, rate = 25/2%, time = ⅕ year S.I.= Rs. ((4000 X 25 X 1)/(2 X 5 X 100))= Rs. 100

questions.question: K started a business with the capital of ₹6000, after 5 months, L started with the capital of ₹4000 and after 8 months from starting, K withdraws ₹2500. If total profit received after one year is ₹16200, then find the share of K.
For a prime number p, the number p√p is:
questions.explanation: Let p be a prime number. Assume p√p is rational. Then (p√p)/p = √p is rational. But √p is irrational for any prime p (since p is not a perfect square). So, the assumption is false. Therefore, p√p is irrational.

questions.question: A line passes through the point (7, -4) and has slope -1. What is the equation of the line?
questions.explanation: Equation of a line passing through the point (x₁, y₁) and having slope m => y - y₁ = m(x - x₁) Here, x₁ = 7, y₁ = -4, and m = -1 => y - (-4) = -1(x - 7) => y + 4 = -x + 7 => y = -x + 3

questions.question: A rectangular hall 50 m by 30 m is to be paved at Rs. 15 per m², except for a circular fountain of radius 3 m at the center. What is the cost of paying? (Use π = 22/7).
questions.explanation: Area of the hall = 50 m X 30 m = 1500 m². Area of the fountain = πr² = (22/7) X 3² = 198/7 ≈ 28.29 m². Area to be paved = Area of hall - Area of fountain = 1500 - 28.29 = 1471.71 m². Total paving cost = Paved area X Cost per m² = 1471.71 X 15 = Rs. 22,075.71 ≈ Rs. 22,075

questions.question: L can complete one-sixth of a certain task in 4⅔ days, M can complete half of the task in 21 days and N can complete 40% of the task in 42 days. L and M work together for 12 days. In how many days can N complete the remaining work alone?
questions.explanation: L can complete one-sixth of a certain task in 4(⅔) days, i.e., he will complete the full task in 6 * (14/3) = 28 days. M can complete half of the task in 21 days, i.e., he will complete the full task in 2 * 21 = 42 days N can complete 40% of the task in 42 days, i.e., he will complete the full task in 42/0.4 = 105 days Let the total work be 420 units (LCM of 28, 42 and 105) So, work done by L per day = 420/28 = 15 units Similarly, work done by M and N per day = 10 units and 4 units respectively In 12 days, work done by L and M together = 12 * (15 + 10) = 300 units Remaining work = 420 - 300 = 120 units No. of days taken by N = 120/4 = 30 days

questions.question: If a + b + c = 3, and ab + bc + ca = abc = -12, then what is the value of a³ + b³ + c³?
questions.explanation: Given, (a + b + c) = 3 Square both sides, a² + b² + c² + 2(ab + bc + ca) = 9 a² + b² + c² + 2 * (-12) = 9 a² + b² + c² = 9 + 24 = 33...(1) We know that, a³ + b³ + c³ - 3abc = (a + b + c)(a² + b² + c² - (ab + bc + ca)) a³ + b³ + c³ - 3 * (-12) = 3 * (33 - (-12)) [from eq.(1)] a³ + b³ + c³ + 36 = 3 * 45 a³ + b³ + c³ = 135 - 36 a³ + b³ + c³ = 99.

questions.question: Which is the greatest among? (√14 + √2), (√7 + √4), (√28 + √1)
questions.explanation: When we multiply both terms without square root in each given option, we get the same number 28. In this case, whose option has the greatest sum of both terms, will be the greatest. Option a: sum is 16. Option b: sum is 11. Option c: sum is 29. Hence greatest will be (√28 + √1).

questions.question: If 25t² − 10t + 1 = 0, find the value of 625t² + (1/t)².
questions.explanation: Let t be a nonzero real number. Divide the given equation by t: 25t − 10 + 1/t = 0. 25t + 1/t = 10. Formula: (25t + 1/t)² = 625t² + 2*25*(t*1/t) + 1/t² = 625t² + 50 + 1/t². Substitute 25t + 1/t = 10 → left side = 10² = 100. 625t² + 1/t² = 100 − 50 = 50.

questions.question: A rectangular sheet has one side of 9 cm and a diagonal of 41 cm. What are the length of the other side and the area of the sheet?
questions.explanation: For a rectangle, (diagonal)² = (length) ² + (breadth) ² Let the unknown side = l cm 41² = l² + 9² => 1681 = l² + 81 => l² = 1600 => l = 40 cm Area of rectangle = 40 X 9 = 360 cm²

questions.question: Choose the most suitable option to replace the highlighted part of the sentence. The CEO herselfs addressed to the interns personally to provide feedback.
questions.explanation: The grammar concept of "correct use of reflexive pronouns and intensifiers" applies here. The highlighted part is wrong because "herselfs" is not a valid reflexive or intensifier form; reflexive pronouns do not pluralise. Additionally, the verb "address" meaning "speak to" does not require the preposition "to" when followed by a direct object. Thus, the phrase becomes ungrammatical both in form and structure. Option (a) - incorrect Uses "themselves," which mismatches the singular antecedent "CEO." Option (b) - correct Removes the incorrect reflexive, uses "addressed the interns" correctly without a preposition, and preserves original meaning. Option (c) - incorrect Misplaces "herself" (an intensifier should follow the subject), and shifts tense unnecessarily to past perfect. Option (d) - incorrect Uses "itself," which cannot refer to a person. Hence, option (b) is correct. Note: Reflexives ("herself") vs. intensifiers ("the CEO herself") follow positional rules: intensifiers directly follow the noun they emphasise; reflexives must correctly match number and person.

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error: The newly implemented safety regulations (1)/ aim to minimise risks during peak operational hours (2)/ and ensure that no employee is exposed (3)/ to hazardous chemicals without proper protective equipments. (4)
options: and ensure that no employee is exposed (3)
aim to minimise risks during peak operational hours (2)
to hazardous chemicals without proper protective equipments (4)
questions.explanation: The word "equipment" is an uncountable noun in English and should not take a plural form. Thus, "equipments" is incorrect. The correct word is "equipment" for both singular and plural forms. Correct: "..to hazardous chemicals without proper protective equipment." Hence, the error is in part (4). Note: Uncountable Nouns (e.g., furniture, equipment, information) do not take plural forms or "a/an".

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error: Although the committee reviewed all the proposals carefully, (1)/ it failed to consider the financial implications of some suggestions, (2)/ which made them difficult to implement within the allocated budget; (3)/ however, the final report was submitted on time. (4)
options: Although the committee reviewed all the proposals carefully, (1)
it failed to consider the financial implications of some suggestions (2)
which made them difficult to implement within the allocated budget; (3)
however, the final report was submitted on time (4)
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun "which" is incorrectly referring to "some suggestions", but the intended reference is the implementation of suggestions. This creates ambiguity. A correct phrasing would be "making it difficult to implement them within the allocated budget." Hence, the error is in part (3). Corrected sentence: Although the committee reviewed all the proposals carefully, it failed to consider the financial implications of some suggestions, making it difficult to implement them within the allocated budget; however, the final report was submitted on time. Note: Pronouns and relative clauses must clearly refer to the correct antecedent to avoid ambiguity.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of: "Paramount"
questions.explanation: Paramount: more important than anything else; supreme. Predominant: present as the strongest or main element. As per the meanings, both words are similar in meaning. Hence, option (d) is the answer. Ambiguous: doubtful or uncertain. Direful: dreadful; awful; terrible; very bad or unpleasant. Inferior: of little or less importance, value, or merit.

questions.question: Which of the following best captures the central theme of the passage?
options: Human memory functions equally for completed and incomplete tasks.
Unfinished tasks create mental tension that can be used to enhance storytelling.
Psychological theories have no practical application in creative writing.
Storytelling relies only on imagination rather than cognitive principles.
questions.explanation: The passage explains that unfinished tasks create "mental tension that the brain seeks to resolve" (Paragraph 1) and further shows how writers use "tactical open loops" to keep readers engaged (Paragraph 2). It concludes that this psychological principle helps create compelling narratives (Paragraph 3). Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: According to the passage, what happens when a task remains unfinished?
options: It is quickly forgotten as the brain shifts focus to new tasks.
It occupies mental space and continues to demand attention.
It becomes less significant compared to completed activities.
It is stored temporarily and later removed from memory.
questions.explanation: The passage clearly states, "As long as something remains unfinished, it occupies cognitive space and continues to demand attention" (Paragraph 1). This directly explains the effect of incomplete tasks on memory. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: How do writers use "tactical open loops" in storytelling?
options: By providing complete information at the beginning of the narrative.
By repeating key ideas to ensure reader understanding.
By leaving certain elements unresolved to sustain engagement.
By simplifying the storyline to avoid confusion among readers.
questions.explanation: The passage explains that "tactical open loops.. are moments.. where information is intentionally withheld or situations are left unresolved" (Paragraph 2). This keeps readers engaged by creating curiosity. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The word "compelling" in the passage most nearly means:
options: Forcing someone to act against their will.
Attractive in a superficial or decorative manner.
Capable of holding attention in a strong way.
Difficult to understand due to complexity.
questions.explanation: The passage states that stories using this technique are "more compelling and memorable" (Paragraph 3). In this context, "compelling" refers to something that strongly captures and maintains attention. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Identify the type of adverb used in the word "subconsciously" in the sentence: "The brain then works subconsciously to close this gap.."
options: Adverb of time indicating when the action occurs.
Adverb of manner describing how the action is performed.
Adverb of place indicating where the action happens.
Adverb of frequency indicating how often the action occurs.
questions.explanation: The word "subconsciously" describes the manner in which the brain works, that is, how the action is performed. It does not indicate time, place, or frequency. Hence, it is an adverb of manner. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct one-word substitute for: 'Something or someone that is not in its correct historical or chronological time.'
questions.explanation: The correct one-word substitute for 'Something or someone that is not in its correct historical or chronological time' is (b) Anachronism. (a) Antipathy: This refers to a deep-seated feeling of aversion; a strong dislike or hostility. For example, some people have a strong antipathy towards certain types of food. (b) Anachronism: This is the correct answer. It is something or someone that is not in its correct historical or chronological time. For example, a knight in shining armor checking his smartphone would be an anachronism. (c) Paradox: This is a statement or proposition that, despite sound (or apparently sound) reasoning from acceptable premises, leads to a conclusion that seems senseless, logically unacceptable, or self-contradictory. "This statement is false" is a classic paradox. (d) Epitome: This means a person or thing that is a perfect example of a particular quality or type. For example, she was the epitome of elegance.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym to replace the italicised word. Joe's hapless search for fun led him from one disappointment to another.
questions.explanation: 'Hapless' means unfortunate; deserving of pity or sympathy due to experiencing unlucky or adverse circumstances. 'Unlucky' means having or experiencing unfortunate events or outcomes; lacking good fortune. So, the two words can be used as synonyms of each other. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. Constant: Occurring continuously or persistently; unchanging. Fruitful: Producing good or useful results; productive or successful. Tireless: Showing great effort or perseverance; not easily exhausted or fatigued.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Decrepitude
questions.explanation: 'Decrepitude' means '(Noun) The state of being old and in bad condition or poor health'. Options a) and d) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Feebleness' means 'Lacking physical strength, especially as a result of age or illness'. Hence, option c) can also be rejected as it has the same meaning as the word. 'Augment' means 'Make (something) greater by adding to it; increase'. 'Concurrent' means 'Existing, happening, or done at the same time'. Hence, option b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. All employees are required to _____ the annual company meeting.
questions.explanation: The correct option to fill in the blank is "attend." 'Attend' in the given context means to be present at or participate in the annual company meeting. The sentence implies that employees are obligated to participate or be present at the meeting. Other words do not fit the context: 'Stumble' means to trip or fall, or to make a mistake, which is unrelated to participating in a meeting. 'Hesitate' means to pause or show uncertainty, which implies reluctance rather than obligation. 'Pretend' means to feign or simulate, which would imply falsely claiming attendance rather than actually being present. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. The complete sentence is: All employees are required to attend the annual company meeting.

questions.question: In the question given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by four options, each having a word or a phrase which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word or phrase that fits in the blank. It is easy to mistake our conscious experience for an ongoing, accurate ______________ of reality.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'to mistake one thing for another' means to confuse something (conscious experience) with something else (reality) or think that something (conscious experience) is something else (reality). Among the given options, only 'account' (which means a report or description of an event or experience) brings out a meaningful sentence. The other options render the sentence meaningless. 'Accountability' means the fact or condition of being accountable; 'vengeance' means punishment inflicted or retribution exacted for an injury or wrong; 'reprisal' means an act of retaliation. Complete sentence: It is easy to mistake our conscious experience for an ongoing, accurate account of reality. Hence, d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "ADUMBRATE".
questions.explanation: "Adumbrate" means to foreshadow or suggest indistinctly. (a) Illuminate = make clear (b) Reveal = disclose (c) Foreshadow = indicate beforehand (d) Exposit = explain The only synonym is (c); other options express clarity, which is the opposite sense. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Convert the sentence provided below from its active voice structure to passive voice structure. Were the dedicated librarians reorganising dusty archives filled with rare manuscripts during the extended library closure last winter?
options: Dusty archives filled with rare manuscripts were being reorganized during the extended library closure last winter by the dedicated librarians.
Were dusty archives filled with rare manuscripts being reorganized by the dedicated librarians during the extended library closure last winter?
The dedicated librarians were being reorganized dusty archives filled with rare manuscripts during the extended library closure last winter.
During the extended library closure last winter, dusty archives filled with rare manuscripts were reorganized by the dedicated librarians.
questions.explanation: The task involves converting a sentence from active voice to passive voice. Key Rules for Active to Passive Voice When converting active voice to passive voice: Object becomes subject: The direct object ("dusty archives...") becomes the new subject. Verb tense remains: Past continuous ("were reorganizing") becomes "were being reorganized" (passive: be + being + past participle, plural). Agent optional: The original subject ("the dedicated librarians") becomes the agent with "by." Adverbs/modifiers unchanged: "Filled with rare manuscripts," "during the extended library closure last winter," remain. Structure: Maintain plural agreement, sequence, and interrogative inversion. Option b) accurately transforms the structure while retaining the question form and all elements. Option a) loses the interrogative. Option c) creates nonsensical agency. Option d) shifts to declarative past simple. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. The power of narratives in cultural diplomacy lies in their ability to transcend geopolitical boundaries, fostering a deep understanding and appreciation of diverse cultures. B. Narratives, whether in the form of literature, film, or art, possess the unique capacity to convey the intricacies of a society's values, history, and collective identity. C. They serve as bridges between cultures, allowing for the exchange of ideas and the celebration of shared humanity. D. Through storytelling, individuals from different corners of the world can connect on a human level, recognizing shared emotions and universal themes. E. When harnessed strategically, narratives become potent instruments for breaking down stereotypes, dispelling misconceptions, and promoting empathy.
questions.explanation: Sentence A introduces the central idea of the power of narratives in cultural diplomacy, emphasising its ability to transcend geopolitical boundaries and foster understanding of diverse cultures. Sentence B follows by elaborating on the various forms of narratives, such as literature, film, or art, and highlights their unique capacity to convey the intricacies of a society's values, history, and collective identity. Sentence D builds on the concept of narratives by emphasising storytelling as a means for individuals from different parts of the world to connect on a human level. It points out that through storytelling, people can recognise shared emotions and universal themes. Sentence E introduces the strategic aspect of harnessing narratives, stating that when used strategically, narratives become potent instruments for breaking down stereotypes, dispelling misconceptions, and promoting empathy. Finally, sentence C concludes the paragraph by emphasising the role of narratives as bridges between cultures. It states that narratives allow for the exchange of ideas and the celebration of shared humanity, reinforcing the idea that they serve as powerful tools in cultural diplomacy. So, the correct order is ABDEC. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank. The researchers said in the households in Gujarat, where resources were ___________, the use of electricity by men was prioritised.
questions.explanation: Use of 'lesser' or 'more' is incorrect in the given blank as no comparison have been made in the sentence. 'Scary' which means 'frightening; causing fear' does not make any sense in the sentence. Hence, the answer is option c) 'scarce' which means 'insufficient for the demand'.

questions.question: Choose the correct antonym of "PEJORATIVE".
questions.explanation: "Pejorative" means expressing disapproval. (a) Demeaning = insulting (b) Derogatory = insulting (c) Laudatory = praising (d) Disparaging = criticizing Only (c) is opposite. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Spot the correct spelling of the medical specialty dealing with the ear, nose, and throat.
questions.explanation: Otorhinolaryngology: The full medical and surgical specialty focused on treating conditions of the ear, nose, throat, and related structures of the head and neck. "ENT" is a shorter, more common term for both this specialty and the doctors who practice it. It stands for Ear, Nose, and Throat. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who attends to the diseases of the eye.
questions.explanation: 'Oculist' means a medical doctor specializing in the eye, deficiencies of vision requiring correction, and diseases. 'Optician' means a person who tests eyesight and sells spectacles. 'Obstetrician' means a physician who assists women during childbirth. 'Optimum' means the best possible result or the best set of conditions to get good results. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correctly spelt word. Her analysis was praised for its _______ clarity and precision.
questions.explanation: "Perspicuous" means "clearly expressed and easy to understand". Remaining options incorrectly modify consonant patterns. Complete sentence: Her analysis was praised for its perspicuous clarity and precision. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Chew the fat
options: Think or talk reflectively in a friendly and informal way.
To consume excess of fats in one's diet
To skip exercise and balanced diet
questions.explanation: The phrase 'chew the fat' means to think or talk reflectively in a friendly and informal way. The following sentence gives an example of the usage of the phrase in a sentence: I spent this evening with Polly, Jo and mostly with Sarah, chewing the fat. Hence, a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence: The old gardener said, "I have been tending to these beautiful flowers in this magnificent garden for more than twenty years with utmost care and dedication."
options: The old gardener said that he was tending to those beautiful flowers in that magnificent garden for more than twenty years with utmost care and dedication.
The old gardener said that he has been tending to these beautiful flowers in that magnificent garden for more than twenty years with utmost care and dedication.
The old gardener said that he had been tending to those beautiful flowers in that magnificent garden for more than twenty years with utmost care and dedication.
The old gardener said that he had tended to those beautiful flowers in that magnificent garden for more than twenty years with utmost care and dedication.
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Direct Speech is in Present Perfect Continuous Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Indirect Speech should be in Past Perfect Continuous Tense. Present Perfect Continuous (Subject + has/have + been + V1 + ing + Object) should be converted into Past Perfect Continuous (Subject + had + been + V1 + ing + Object). For example: He said, "We have been waiting for hours." (Direct Speech) He said that they had been waiting for hours. (Indirect Speech) The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: · I changes to he. · have been tending changes to had been tending. · these changes to those. · this changes to that. Therefore, the correct answer is: "The old gardener said that he had been tending to those beautiful flowers in that magnificent garden for more than twenty years with utmost care and dedication." Hence, option (c) is correct. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: The figure of speech used in "Time flies like an arrow; fruit flies like a banana" is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (a) Pun. A pun is a figure of speech that plays on words to produce a humorous or witty effect by exploiting multiple meanings or similar sounds. In this example, the phrase "flies like" is used in two different senses - first as a verb phrase (time flies like an arrow meaning time passes quickly), and then as a noun phrase (fruit flies like a banana referring to the insect fruit flies). The clever double meaning creates humor. Metaphor (option b) makes a direct comparison between two unlike things (e.g., "Time is a thief"), which is not the case here. Personification (option c) attributes human qualities to non-human things (e.g., "The wind sang through the trees"), which does not occur in this example. Paradox (option d) presents a seemingly self-contradictory statement that reveals a deeper truth (e.g., "The beginning of the end"), which is not applicable here.
